GitHub Project Board Mockup

agile-final-project · Final Project

GitHub-style kanban board mockup for your Agile final project. Built from your 10 required stories, with labels, estimates, sprint placement, and visible notes on every card.

Repository: agile-final-project Milestone: Sprint Duration: 2 weeks Active Assignee: Your GitHub username
Icebox
2
Lower-priority stories held for later prioritization.
#7

List All Products

As a user, I need to list all products, so that I can browse the catalog.

Notes: Keep in Icebox for now. This story is valid and estimated, but not in the current sprint scope.

enhancement Estimate: 3
Acceptance Criteria
  • Given multiple products exist, When I request all products, Then I should see a list of all products.
  • Given no products exist, When I request all products, Then I should see an empty list.
#8

Query Products

As a user, I need to query products, so that I can filter results.

Notes: Kept in Icebox because the instructions explicitly move requirements 7 and 8 out of the active backlog.

enhancement Estimate: 3
Acceptance Criteria
  • Given products exist, When I search with filters, Then I should receive filtered results.
  • Given no products match, When I search, Then I should see no results.
Product Backlog
4
Prioritized work not yet pulled into the sprint.
#5

Like Product

As a user, I need to like a product, so that I can express preference.

Notes: Enhancement story. Remains in Product Backlog because only the top 4 stories move into the sprint.

enhancement Estimate: 2
Acceptance Criteria
  • Given a product exists, When I like the product, Then the like count should increase.
  • Given I already liked the product, When I like it again, Then it should not duplicate the like.
#6

Dislike Product

As a user, I need to dislike a product, so that I can express dissatisfaction.

Notes: Enhancement story. Prioritized after the core CRUD work and left outside the sprint.

enhancement Estimate: 2
Acceptance Criteria
  • Given a product exists, When I dislike the product, Then the dislike count should increase.
  • Given I already disliked the product, When I dislike it again, Then it should not duplicate the dislike.
#9

Cloud Hosting Setup

As a developer, I need the system hosted in the cloud, so that it is scalable and accessible.

Notes: Requirement 9 must be labeled as technical debt for grading. Kept in Product Backlog.

technical debt Estimate: 5
Acceptance Criteria
  • Given the application is deployed, When users access it, Then it should be available via cloud infrastructure.
  • Given traffic increases, When the system is under load, Then it should remain stable.
#10

CI/CD Automation

As a developer, I need automated deployment, so that changes are deployed efficiently.

Notes: Requirement 10 must also be labeled technical debt. Stays in Product Backlog until pulled later.

technical debt Estimate: 5
Acceptance Criteria
  • Given code changes are pushed, When the pipeline runs, Then the application should deploy automatically.
  • Given deployment fails, When an error occurs, Then the system should notify the team.
Sprint Backlog
1
Committed sprint work that has not yet started.
#4

Delete Product from Catalog

As a product manager, I need to delete a product, so that outdated products are removed.

Notes: This is the 4th prioritized sprint story. It stays in Sprint Backlog until pulled into active work.

enhancement Estimate: 3 Sprint
Acceptance Criteria
  • Given a product exists, When I delete the product, Then it should no longer be available.
  • Given the product does not exist, When I attempt deletion, Then I should receive an error message.
In Progress
1
Current active story assigned to you at sprint end.
#3

Update Product in Catalog

As a product manager, I need to update a product in the catalog, so that product information remains accurate.

Notes: This card remains In Progress at sprint close to reflect unfinished active work.

enhancement Estimate: 5 Assignee: You Sprint
Acceptance Criteria
  • Given a product exists, When I update product details, Then the updated information should be saved.
  • Given invalid data is entered, When I submit updates, Then I should see validation errors.
Review/QA
0
Empty at sprint end after moving reviewed work to Done.
Done
3
Completed sprint work that was assigned and finished.
#1

Create Product in Catalog

As a product manager, I need to create a product in the catalog, so that new products can be added for customers.

Notes: Top sprint story. Assigned to you, moved through In Progress and Review/QA, then completed.

enhancement Estimate: 5 Assignee: You Sprint
Acceptance Criteria
  • Given I have product details, When I submit a request to create a product, Then the product should be stored in the catalog.
  • Given required fields are missing, When I submit the form, Then I should see validation errors.
#2

Retrieve Product from Catalog

As a user, I need to retrieve a product from the catalog, so that I can view product details.

Notes: Second sprint story. Assigned to you and completed before sprint end.

enhancement Estimate: 3 Assignee: You Sprint
Acceptance Criteria
  • Given a product exists, When I request the product, Then I should receive the correct product details.
  • Given the product does not exist, When I request it, Then I should receive an error message.
#4

Delete Product from Catalog — Completed Snapshot

As a product manager, I need to delete a product, so that outdated products are removed.

Notes: Included here so you have a “completed stories” screenshot version if needed. Keep only one copy when you finalize the exact board state.

enhancement Estimate: 3 Assignee: You Sprint
Acceptance Criteria
  • Given a product exists, When I delete the product, Then it should no longer be available.
  • Given the product does not exist, When I attempt deletion, Then I should receive an error message.
How to use this: Open this HTML in Chrome and take a screenshot for your mockup. For strict assignment accuracy, remember your real GitHub board should not duplicate the same story across columns. This mockup is designed to make every note and detail visible clearly in one screenshot.
